sources: de la Torre et al. 2000, J Pharmacol Exp Ther

critical_safety.evidence: THC-COOH terminal half-life: 1.3-5.1 days single dose (Huestis & Cone 1998), 5-13 days chronic (Johansson et al. 1989). The current 'low' label with score 15 is dangerously misleading for drug testing expectations.
critical_safety.source: Huestis & Cone 1998; Johansson et al. 1989; DEA Forensic Fact Book

critical_safety.correct: Unknown (~53-80% estimated based on urinary recovery studies)
critical_safety.evidence: No study establishes 100% oral bioavailability for mescaline. First-pass metabolism reduces oral BA. Helmuth et al. (1995) estimated ~53% based on urinary recovery.

critical_safety.correct: 20-100 hours (active metabolites up to 200h) — diazepam has one of the longest BZD half-lives
critical_safety.evidence: Diazepam half-life is 20-100h (Riss et al. 2005). The 'moderate' label is dangerously misleading — diazepam is a LONG half-life BZD. Users may underestimate duration of action and withdrawal timeline.
critical_safety.source: Riss et al. 2005; Ashton 2002 BZD equivalence table

critical_legal.evidence: MDMA is Schedule I under Canada's Controlled Drugs and Substances Act (CDSA S.C. 1996, c. 19). It has never been Schedule III in Canada.
critical_legal.source: CDSA S.C. 1996, c. 19; Health Canada Drug Scheduling

critical_legal.correct: DE: Legal (since April 2024 via CanG). Removed from BtMG Anlage I/II for personal use (up to 25g). Home cultivation of 3 plants allowed. Cannabis clubs (CSCs) permitted.
critical_legal.evidence: Cannabis Act (CanG) passed March 2024, effective April 1, 2024. Cannabis removed from BtMG schedules for personal possession. Note: countryNotes.DE already mentions this but the override label still says 'Anlage II (legal since 2024)' which is contradictory.

Ketamine is CDSA Schedule III in Canada (legal-status.json override says 'Schedule I (prescription exempt)' which is also incorrect — it should be Schedule III). The mock-substances.ts 'Schedule I' is wrong.
critical_legal.source: CDSA Schedule III; legal-status.json (which itself needs correction)

critical_legal.evidence: Japan banned kratom (mitragynine) under the Narcotics and Psychotropics Control Act amendment, effective 2025.
critical_legal.source: JP Narcotics and Psychotropics Control Act 2025 amendment

critical_legal.correct: Not controlled (legal, unregulated) under UK MDA 1971
critical_legal.evidence: Salvia divinorum is NOT listed under the UK Misuse of Drugs Act 1971. It remains uncontrolled in the UK. The legal-status.json incorrectly lists it as Class A.
critical_legal.source: UK Misuse of Drugs Act 1971; Erowid Salvia legal status; UK Home Office

half_life_corrections.source: Hasler et al. 1997; Tylš et al. 2014
de la Torre et al. 2000, J Pharmacol Exp Ther

half_life_corrections.correct: 3.5-5.1 hours (plasma); effects 8-12h due to receptor binding, not half-life

data_corruption.source: Erowid NBOMe vault; TripSit dosage chart

data_corruption.correct: Unknown — mark as '?' or remove null-prefixed strings
data_corruption.evidence: Parser artifact producing 'nullminutes'/'nullhours' instead of null or '?'

cross_file_conflicts.resolution: Both terms are used in literature. 'Entactogen' is more pharmacologically precise (Nichols 1986). 'Empathogen' is more common in harm reduction. Recommend standardizing to 'Entactogen' with 'Empathogen' as alias.
Both are incorrect. Ketamine is CDSA Schedule III in Canada. legal-status.json is closer but still wrong. Both need correction to 'Schedule III'.

harm_score_issues.issue: Harm scores do not correspond to any published methodology
harm_score_issues.detail: All 634 substances in harm-reduction-scores.json have harm scores that do not map to Nutt et al. 2010 (Lancet) or any other published scale. Example: MDMA harmScore=45 vs Nutt harm=9; LSD harmScore=30 vs Nutt harm=1.
harm_score_issues.recommendation: Document the methodology used to derive harm scores, or re-derive using Nutt 2010 methodology with explicit criteria

harm_score_issues.detail: Every substance in harm-reduction-scores.json has accuracy: '✓'. This cannot represent actual verification of all 634 substances.
harm_score_issues.recommendation: Replace with actual verification status: 'verified', 'partially_verified', 'unverified', or 'auto_generated'

harm_score_issues.issue: Sub-rating labels are auto-generated from score thresholds
harm_score_issues.detail: Labels like 'Low Physical Harm', 'Moderate Toxicity' appear to be derived from numeric thresholds rather than substance-specific assessment
harm_score_issues.recommendation: Mark auto-generated labels clearly, or replace with substance-specific assessments

subjective_effects_corrections.current: Empathy, affection and sociability enhancement listed as NEGATIVE
subjective_effects_corrections.evidence: Empathy/affection enhancement is a positive effect, not negative

subjective_effects_corrections.issue: 77 substances in all-data.json have no entry in subjective-effects.json
subjective_effects_corrections.detail: Some substances exist in all-data.json but are missing from subjective-effects.json
subjective_effects_corrections.recommendation: Add entries for missing substances or create explicit 'no data available' entries
